arts.ttl: Live updates: Sam Smith and Phil Parkinson return as Reading host Wrexham
arts.sum: Reading host big-spending Wrexham at the SCL Stadium as Phil Parkinson and Sam Smith return to Berkshire. The North Wales outfit can go clear in the automatic promotion race with a win, but the Royals need three points to keep up with the play-offs.

arts.ttl: Reading team news: Chem Campbell returns from the start for Wrexham meeting
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has made two changes for the visit of Wrexham AFC, with Chem Campbell returning from the start. The Wolves loanee missed the last two home matches and was a second half substitute for Saturday's 1-1 draw with Crawley Town. However, for the visit of promotion-chasing Wrexham, Hunt has opted to replace both wingers. Campbell and Jayden Wareham come in, with Kelvin Ehibhatiomhan likely to go out wide with Wareham through the middle.

arts.ttl: Former Reading coach replaces long-time colleague at struggling Tranmere Rovers
arts.sum: Former Reading coach Andy Crosby has replaced his long-time colleague Nigel Adkins at Tranmere Rovers. Crosby was Adkins' assistant at Scunthorpe United, Southampton, Reading, Sheffield United and Hull City before taking on managerial roles of his own at Port Vale. Adkins, who was at Reading between 2013 and 2014, was sacked by his hometown club with Tranmere sat 22nd out of 24 in League Two, four points clear of the relegation zone. Named interim, Crosby will continue until at least the end of the current campaign.

arts.ttl: Former Reading youngster returns to league football after 10 months out
arts.sum: Former Reading defender Michael Hector has returned to football after 10 months out with Dagenham and Redbridge. The 32-year-old graduated from the Royals Academy and went on to make nearly 80 appearances, earning a big move to Chelsea in 2015. Failing to make an impact at Stamford Bridge, the Jamaica international had four loan spells away from West London, including a spell with Eintracht Frankfurt in the Bundesliga.

arts.ttl: ‘Top of his game’ – Hull City man hailed by Ruben Selles ahead of Oxford United clash
arts.sum: Hull City boss Ruben Selles has said Gustavo Puerta is playing at the ‘top of his game’ at… The post ‘Top of his game’ – Hull City man hailed by Ruben Selles ahead of Oxford United clash appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: ‘Give him credit’ – Wrexham boss Phil Parkinson reacts to injury boost
arts.sum: Wrexham boss Phil Parkinson has said George Evans deserves ‘credit’ following his return from injury. Wrexham brought on… The post ‘Give him credit’ – Wrexham boss Phil Parkinson reacts to injury boost appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Reading prepare to host Wrexham for first time since victory on route to promotion
arts.sum: Reading are hosting Wrexham on Tuesday for the first time since a 2-0 victory in November 2001. Alan Pardew's side completed the league double over the Red Dragons on route to promotion to the second tier, a season in which the Welsh side suffered relegation to the fourth tier. The last time Reading visited Berkshire was November 6, 2001 and goals from Nicky Forster and Darius Henderson completed the victory. Reading's current Head of Recruitment, Brian Carey, was playing for the visitors and made over 200 appearances for the club, even enjoying a spell as their manager. For Wrexham, Sam Smith, Phil Parkinson and George Evans will all be returning to their former homes.

arts.ttl: Crawley Town boss references 'poor' pitch in 'scrappy' draw with Reading
arts.sum: Crawley Town boss Rob Elliott spoke of a 'sticky pitch' in Saturday's draw, which lead to a 'scrappy' 1-1 draw at the Broadfield Stadium. Kelvin Ehibhatiomhan opened the scoring in the first half and the Royals looked set to keep chase with the play-offs sides but Pantuche Camara tapped home a scruffy 89th-minute equaliser to earn a share of the spoils. It could have been better for the hosts as the Red Devils had the ball in the net shortly afterwards, only for referee Craig Breakspear to blow up for full time as the ball was mid-flight towards the box. Speaking after the match, Elliott admitted that the pitch did not allow the match to be played in the manner that both teams would have wanted.

arts.ttl: Reading supporters to stage protest against Dai Yongge as Wrexham come to town
arts.sum: Fans of Reading FC and Wrexham AFC will stage a protest during the match between the two teams tomorrow night (11 March, 8pm kick-off) to draw attention to the ongoing ownership crisis that has left Reading in turmoil.

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers and Phil Parkinson have same opinion on ex-Man Utd star shining at Wrexham
arts.sum: Wrexham boss Phil Parkinson has hailed the impact of former Manchester United and Leicester midfielder Matty James, who has been a key player for the League One side
